reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: worker.js loads the tokenizer + model MANUALLY (AutoTokenizer + AutoModelForMaskedLM from Xenova/albert-base-v2, dtype q8, device wasm) rather than the fill-mask pipeline, so the See-inside surface reads the EXACT masked-token logits at each [MASK] and softmaxes them in the worker. Conformance calls-real-inference (source) + loads-clean (console-clean) tested pass; io-shape-semantic manual-evidenced. No measured latency fabricated.
rubric.notes: ALBERT base v2: a 'lite' BERT reusing ONE transformer block across all 12 layers (cross-layer parameter sharing) + factorised embedding (vocab→128→768) — ~11.8M params vs BERT-base's ~110M, hence the tiny ~45 MB download.

rubric.evidence: For each [MASK], worker.js returns top-k {tokenId, token, logit, prob} from the real logits row; supports fill / fillMany / scoreCandidates. SentencePiece decode surfaces the ▁ word-boundary marker, cleaned via cleanToken() for display. io-shape-semantic conformance assertion covers real per-mask predictions.
rubric.notes: cleanToken replaces ▁ with a space and trims — a display fix specific to ALBERT's SentencePiece tokenizer (vs BERT's WordPiece ## prefix).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares Xenova/albert-base-v2 (linked, 5 occurrences), the fill-mask task, q8 dtype, WASM backend, ~45 MB and Apache-2.0 license — matching models.json. declares-model-id/task/backend/quantisation conformance assertions tested pass.
rubric.notes: Smallest model in this NLP batch (~45 MB). Inference off the main thread; WASM everywhere, no WebGPU needed.

rubric.evidence: The page uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init (modelId Xenova/albert-base-v2, q8, ~45 MB, requiresWebGPU:false); a valid current cached copy auto-initialises with no Load click. shared-auto-init-loader conformance assertion tested pass.
rubric.notes: env.allowLocalModels=false so the library owns Cache Storage (no SW double-store). At ~45 MB the first download is quick even on mobile.

rubric.evidence: runStatus role=status aria-live; worker errors surface on the page; shared loader adds checking→initialising→ready + Retry. live-status-region + errors-on-the-page tested pass; honest-progress-retry-offline / runs-without-webgpu-fallback manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: scoreCandidates is a graceful no-op (empty scores) when the text has no [MASK].

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled controls: the masked-text input, a top-k range slider, a fill button, and the candidate-scoring surface. All keyboard-operable. controls-operable conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Note: firstTokenId uses the v3 encode signature encode(word, {add_special_tokens:false}) — the worker comment records that passing null as the second arg (the old signature) crashes reading text_pair, so the code deliberately uses the correct options-object form. A real, fixed footgun.

rubric.evidence: No canned predictions: every token, logit and probability is computed from the real logits row. no-fake-output conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
fill button + controls carry names (buttons-have-names tested pass); status role=status aria-live; results aria-live; slider labelled; page lang=en; WCAG-AA light+dark. images-have-alt tested pass.
rubric.notes: Prediction bars are DOM with text logits/probabilities.

rubric.evidence: responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) conformance assertions present; design-system panels + flex-wrap prediction rows. models.json support is needs-review on both classes (automated pre-scan flagged sub-24px tap targets), pending an agent matrix pass to confirm slider/button tap sizes and flip to ok.
Forward pass + top-k/softmax over the vocab run in the Web Worker; the main thread only renders bars. inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: The tiny model makes runs fast; the work is off-thread and bounded so INP stays low.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run the forward pass and vocab-wide softmax/top-k off the main thread; keep INP low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference and the top-k/softmax run in the worker; the main thread only paints prediction bars.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread; inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form labels text input range slider
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control; echo slider value; keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — masked-text input, top-k slider and candidate controls are labelled and keyboard-operable.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce state via role=status aria-live; give controls accessible names.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — runStatus role=status aria-live; results aria-live; fill button named.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: live-status-region + buttons-have-names conformance assertions tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid layout with wrapping; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — design-system panels; prediction rows and candidate chips flex-wrap.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive assertions present; support needs-review pending agent matrix pass

openQuestions: Multi-token candidates are compared by first subword only (disclosed) — worth an optional full-word scoring mode for fairness?
ALBERT-base is tiny (~11.8M params); should the page contrast its predictions with the larger BERT demo so users see the capacity trade-off directly?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a side-by-side ALBERT-vs-BERT fill-mask comparison (they share the demo shell + manual-tokenizer path) so users can see the lite-model capacity trade-off on the same masked prompt.
Run the mobile+desktop responsive matrix, confirm/raise slider and button tap targets, and flip models.json support to ok.

summary: Retrospective, code-grounded revision-1 critique for albert-fill-mask (Xenova/albert-base-v2, fill-mask, q8, ~45 MB, Apache-2.0 — the smallest model in this NLP batch, ~11.8M params via cross-layer parameter sharing). Real masked-LM: worker.js loads AutoModelForMaskedLM directly so the See-inside surface reads the EXACT masked-token logits and softmaxes them in the worker (with a SentencePiece ▁-marker cleanup for display); supports fill, fillMany and scoreCandidates. Auto-init via createModelLoader (env.allowLocalModels=false, no SW double-store); role=status aria-live states; no fake output. The code also records a real fixed footgun: firstTokenId uses the correct v3 encode(word,{add_special_tokens:false}) signature. Verified against the immutable conformance suite (calls-real-inference, loads-clean, declares-*, inference-off-main-thread, shared-auto-init-loader, live-status-region, buttons-have-names tested pass; io-shape/parity/inp manual-evidenced). No fabricated numbers. Honest weaknesses: first-subword-only candidate comparison (disclosed); tiny model capacity; models.json support needs-review pending a tap-target matrix pass.
